candidate.title: AWS Trainium2 Trn2 aggregate-memory catalog promotion

local_checks.raw_manifest_scan: Found and reused the existing local AWS Trn2 official instance-page capture under raw/crawler/compute-accelerators-aws-trn2. No external fetch, link probe, crawler source subscription, or Domain Channels change was performed.
local_checks.wiki_search: Searched the compute accelerator catalog, parameter comparison, coverage map, network/storage page, data catalog rows, coverage-map.json, loop-state.json, ingest.md, and prior manifests for Trainium2, Trn2, trn2.48xlarge, trn2u.48xlarge, UltraServer, aggregate_memory, 1.5 TB, 1536, 6 TB, and 6144. The prior structured row had only cloud_accelerator_count=16, while aggregate_memory was not resolved.
local_checks.domain_index_scan: ai_infra wiki/index.md already exposes Compute Accelerator Spec Catalog, Compute Accelerator Parameter Comparison, and AI Infra Coverage Map. The task updates existing pages and structured data only.
local_checks.prior_manifest_scan: Prior manifests cite AWS Trn2 as EFA/cloud-offering boundary evidence or an aggregate row excluded from earlier product-specific slices. This task is the first parent-17 promotion of aggregate_memory for the existing aws-trainium2-trn2-offering row.

gap_reason: Parent-17 has a concrete non-duplicate catalog gap: the AWS Trn2 local raw source states 1.5 TB accelerator memory for the two 16-chip Trn2 instance sizes, the catalog schema supports aggregate_memory for cloud offerings, and the existing Trn2 resolved row had not represented that field.

duplicate_checks.finding: Before the task, aws-trainium2-trn2-offering resolved cloud_accelerator_count only. After the task it resolves aggregate_memory=1536 GB from obs-aws-trn2-aggregate-memory.
Added one source-backed S2 observation for aggregate_memory and did not add aggregate compute, memory bandwidth, EFAv3, storage, cost, service, testimonial, or UltraServer observations.
The AWS Trn2 resolved row now shows 16 accelerators and 1536 GB aggregate accelerator memory; the UltraServer row remains comparison-only boundary evidence.
Catalog coverage records parent-17 as a bounded cloud-offering aggregate-memory promotion on the existing Trn2 row.
Hardware coverage records the parent-17 AWS Trn2 aggregate-memory update while preserving unresolved aggregate-system and fabric boundaries.
Known sources now include parent-17 AWS Trainium2 Trn2 aggregate memory without changing unrelated blocked items.

selected_source_backed_signals.promoted_claim: The `trn2.48xlarge` and `trn2u.48xlarge` rows each list 16 Trainium2 chips and 1.5 TB accelerator memory; the catalog records aggregate_memory as 1536 GB for the existing Trn2 offering row.

rejected_or_deferred_signals.item: Trn2 UltraServer aggregate memory and chip count
rejected_or_deferred_signals.reason: Not added as a separate structured row in this task because the safe primary promotion is the existing Trn2 instance cloud-offering row; UltraServer values remain comparison-only boundary evidence.

rejected_or_deferred_signals.item: Aggregate FP8 PFLOPS and total memory bandwidth
rejected_or_deferred_signals.reason: Not promoted because catalog compute and memory_bandwidth fields are per accelerator unless the field name explicitly says aggregate.

rejected_or_deferred_signals.item: EFAv3 bandwidth and local NVMe storage
rejected_or_deferred_signals.reason: Not promoted because EFAv3 is cloud fabric evidence rather than DPU/IPU network_bandwidth, and local NVMe is outside the accelerator field schema.

rejected_or_deferred_signals.item: Price-performance, energy-efficiency, model count, service integration, availability, testimonials, and production wording
rejected_or_deferred_signals.reason: Not promoted because the task is limited to source-visible structured accelerator fields, not cost, service availability, benchmark, SLO, or production-operation claims.

non_promoted_boundaries: No Trn2 UltraServer structured row was added.
No UltraServer 6 TB memory, 64 Trainium2 chips, 83.2 FP8 PFLOPS, 185 TBps memory bandwidth, 12.8 Tbps EFAv3, NeuronLink, or availability wording was promoted as a resolved catalog field.
No aggregate FP8 PFLOPS was promoted into fp8_tflops, no total memory bandwidth was promoted into memory_bandwidth, and no EFAv3 bandwidth was promoted into network_bandwidth.

No local NVMe storage, EBS bandwidth, vCPU count, host memory, price-performance, energy-efficiency, model count, customer testimonial, service integration, benchmark, production-operation, service-SLO, training-throughput, or inference-throughput claim was promoted.
No new external source was fetched, and no crawler subscriptions or Domain Channels were changed.
